demographics.living_situation: foster care placement with a single foster carer

opening_complaint.verbatim_patient_text: He tore his room apart, hit me with a chair, and keeps trying to unlock the front door to run into the street. I can't calm him down and I'm exhausted.
opening_complaint.stated_reason_for_presenting_today: He's been completely out of control for about three hours since dinner was a bit late, and he keeps trying to get out the front door toward the road.

symptom_narrative.onset: about three hours ago, right after dinner was served roughly 15 minutes late
symptom_narrative.character: screaming, extremely hyperactive, throwing and breaking things, hitting out, and repeatedly trying to unlock the door and run outside
symptom_narrative.severity_patient_description: the worst meltdown she has ever seen from him; she cannot redirect or calm him at all
symptom_narrative.timing_pattern: constant since it started, no let-up
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_better: nothing so far — his usual calming strategies and the as-needed melatonin have not helped
symptom_narrative.what_makes_it_worse: any attempt to stop him, and the change to his usual routine
symptom_narrative.associated_symptoms_volunteered: he is breathing fast and seems like his heart is racing, and he is sweaty and flushed from all the activity
symptom_narrative.previous_episodes: he has meltdowns from time to time but she has never seen one this severe or this long
symptom_narrative.functional_impact: he cannot keep himself safe at all right now and is completely dependent on her for safety

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: fetal alcohol spectrum disorder (FASD) — his birth mother drank alcohol during the pregnancy
past_medical_history.how_managed: specialist and behavioural supports plus a daily medication

past_medical_history.condition_as_patient_states: developmental delay, with a lot of trouble controlling his impulses, attention and emotions

family_history_as_reported.narrative: his birth mother drank alcohol during the pregnancy; he has been in foster care

social_history_volunteered.relevant_life_context: 8-year-old boy in a foster placement; the foster mother is the only carer present and is physically exhausted after three hours

paediatric_context.developmental_concerns: documented global developmental delay with prominent difficulties in impulse control, attention and emotional regulation

objective_data_offered.value: pacing rapidly, screaming, loud and disorganised speech, cannot be calmed or redirected

the front door is barricaded with a heavy couch; the living room is disrupted with thrown and broken objects; he has tried to unlock the door and run toward the road several times in the last three hours
he is breathing fast and feels hot and sweaty, and his heart seems to be pounding when she holds him
objective_data_offered.reliability_caveat: the carer's impression only — not a measured reading
